name: Argo CD API
what_it_does: Argo CD REST API — manage GitOps-driven Kubernetes deployments programmatically: sync applications, monitor health status, manage rollbacks, and query cluster state.
use_cases: Agents triggering application syncs after new container images are pushed to a registry
Monitoring deployment health — agents polling Argo CD for sync status and Kubernetes health checks
Rolling back deployments — agents reverting to previous application revision on detected failures
Multi-cluster deployments — agents deploying to multiple Kubernetes clusters via Argo CD app sets
Image updater integration — agents updating kustomize overlays or Helm values to trigger new deploys
not_for: Teams not using Kubernetes — Argo CD requires a Kubernetes cluster to deploy to
Non-GitOps workflows — if you push directly rather than via Git, use kubectl or Helm directly
Simple Docker deployments — use Coolify, Render, or Fly.io for simpler container deployments
best_when: Your agent needs to manage Kubernetes application deployments in a GitOps workflow, triggering syncs, checking health, and managing rollbacks programmatically.
avoid_when: You're not using Kubernetes or GitOps — Argo CD's value is specifically in the Git-as-source-of-truth model.

auth.notes: JWT tokens obtained via username/password or SSO login. Local users or OIDC SSO (Okta, Dex, GitHub). API keys (long-lived tokens) for service accounts. RBAC policies control resource access.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Completely free and open source (Apache 2.0); self-hosted
pricing.paid_tiers: Akuity (managed Argo CD): from $49/month; Codefresh: enterprise pricing
pricing.notes: Self-hosted Argo CD is free. Managed options like Akuity or Codefresh add cost but reduce operational burden.

performance.rate_limits: No documented rate limits for self-hosted; Kubernetes API server is the practical bottleneck

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API errors follow standard HTTP status codes. Sync operation errors require polling operation status — initial sync call returns immediately. Kubernetes-level errors surface through Argo CD status objects.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Sync operations are idempotent — triggering sync on already-synced app is safe. Application creation with same name returns error (already exists).
known_agent_gotchas: JWT tokens expire — agents must refresh tokens before expiry or handle 401 to re-authenticate
Sync is async — POST /sync returns immediately; agents must poll application status for sync completion
RBAC policy changes require Argo CD restart to take effect in some versions
ApplicationSet resources require additional permissions beyond basic Application CRUD
Self-signed TLS certificates common in self-hosted installs — agents must configure TLS verification accordingly

security_score_components.security_notes: RBAC with project and resource-level policies. SSO via OIDC/Dex. Self-hosted: TLS depends on your configuration. Kubernetes secrets used for repository credentials. CNCF graduated project with active security audits.
